findings.evidence: `rg -n "normalizerQuotientEquivMap_trans|conjMulEquivTrans|subgroup_map_conj_trans"` shows only `normalizerQuotientEquivMap_trans_mk` at line 150, while the existing deck API has full composite lemmas at `Deck/Conjugation.lean:165` and `:181`.
findings.fix: Add characteristic composition and identity lemmas, preferably also deck-specialized versions using `Deck.conjMulEquivTrans`, `subgroup_map_conj_trans`, and `subgroup_map_conj_refl`, so users can rewrite transports to the composite/refl transport without unfolding.
findings.issue: `normalizerQuotientEquivMap_trans_mk` only rewrites to the iterated target `normalizerQuotient ((H.map e).map f)` and never characterizes it against transport by `e.trans f`; downstream conjugacy bookkeeping must still unfold/cast through `Subgroup.map_map`.

findings.evidence: `rg -n "normalizerQuotientEquivMap_mk|normalizerQuotientEquivMap_mk_coe|normalizerQuotientConjEquiv_mk|normalizerQuotientConjEquiv_mk_coe"` reports duplicate simp LHS pairs at lines 123/170 and deck lines 55/123.
findings.fix: Keep one simp normal form; make the `_coe` restatement non-simp, or replace the abstract `_mk` simp lemma if the raw-coe representative is intended as the canonical form. Do the same for the deck `_mk_coe` overlap.
findings.issue: `@[simp] normalizerQuotientEquivMap_mk_coe` has the same left-hand side as `@[simp] normalizerQuotientEquivMap_mk`, giving two competing normal forms and letting `simp` expose constructor/proof details instead of the stable `normalizerEquivMap` representative.

summary: The transport maps expose representative formulas, but the characteristic API still does not identify identity/composite transports with the canonical identity/composite maps, and the simp surface has overlapping normal forms.
